explanations.a: A capacitor stores energy in an electric field, not a magnetic field.
explanations.b: A diode controls the direction of current flow but does not store energy.
explanations.c: Correct! An inductor stores energy in the form of a magnetic field when current flows through it.
explanations.d: A resistor dissipates electrical energy as heat instead of storing it.

explanations.a: Correct! A VSWR of 1 indicates perfect impedance matching with no reflected power.
explanations.b: A VSWR of 10 indicates poor impedance matching with high reflections.
explanations.c: A VSWR of 20 represents very poor impedance matching.
explanations.d: A VSWR of 100 indicates extremely poor impedance matching.

questions.question: The operating principle of a slotline is based on:

explanations.a: Correct! A slotline operates by guiding electromagnetic waves along a slot in a conducting plane.
explanations.b: Chemical reactions are unrelated to slotline operation.
explanations.c: Slotlines rely on electromagnetic wave propagation, not only electrostatic charging.
explanations.d: Thermal conduction is not the operating principle of a slotline.

explanations.a: Slotlines are not designed for low-frequency power applications.
explanations.b: Correct! Slotlines are widely used in microwave integrated circuits and RF applications.
explanations.c: Slotlines are not intended for DC circuit applications.
explanations.d: Audio amplifiers do not typically use slotline transmission structures.
